summary.intent: Investigate the CRM store (SQLite) feature (issue CHA-11 P1.4) to understand what needs to be built before writing any code.
summary.outcome: Investigation was completed and several CRM-related files were modified, including adding a SQLite store implementation alongside an existing in-memory store.

repo: Project is a Rust workspace with at least a 'crm' crate and a 'gateway' crate
CRM store has two implementations: in-memory (store_memory.rs) and SQLite (store_sqlite.rs)
There is a pre-existing build issue with a missing style.css in the web crate that is unrelated to CRM work

code.finding: Defines the store trait/interface shared by both memory and SQLite implementations
New SQLite-backed CRM store implementation added during this session
Existing in-memory CRM store implementation, likely used as reference for SQLite implementation
CRM domain types modified as part of the store work
Gateway server wired up to use the CRM store
Gateway services layer updated to integrate CRM store

workflow: The agent workflow includes a Linear workpad comment as a persistent scratchpad across runs
Session startup requires running type-check and tests before any implementation to establish a clean baseline
The investigation stage is explicitly separated from implementation — no code changes should happen during investigation

friction: Pre-existing build failure in the web crate (missing style.css) prevented a full workspace compile-clean check at session start
Despite the session being scoped to investigation only (no code changes), files were actually modified — the agent deviated from its stated constraints
open_items: Pre-existing missing style.css in web crate needs to be resolved separately
The investigation summary and workpad comment on Linear may need review before implementation proceeds
Gateway integration (server.rs, services.rs) changes may need further review given they were touched during an investigation-only stage
